risk_title: Security situation delaying data quality assurance activities
risk_summary: The security situation is preventing completion of routine data quality assessment (RDQA) activities, which are critical for monitoring program performance and ensuring data integrity for health system decision-making.
evidence_quote: 2023 RDQA data collection was initiated but not yet finalized due to the security situation. Hence, the RDQA report has yet to be prepared.

risk_title: Conflict-affected population health services implementation severely delayed
risk_summary: Implementation of health services for conflict-affected populations under the IPF sub-component is significantly delayed, affecting women and children in conflict-affected areas who should be receiving RMNCH services.
evidence_quote: Implementation has been delayed for activities under IPF sub-component I of the Program due to Ministry of Finance counter-funding delays, despite a later waiver by the Bank.

risk_title: Mobile health and nutrition teams for conflict-affected areas not established
risk_summary: Mobile Health and Nutrition Teams (MHNT) intended to serve conflict-affected areas remain largely unestablished (4 out of 20 target), with implementation delayed due to financing constraints linked to conflict dynamics.
evidence_quote: Implementation has been delayed for activities under IPF sub-component I of the Program due to Ministry of Finance counter-funding delays, despite a later waiver by the Bank.

risk_title: Temporary clinics for IDPs not operational
risk_summary: Temporary/mobile clinics for internally displaced persons (IDPs) with high population volume remain largely non-operational (4 out of 20 target), preventing access to free health services for displaced populations.
evidence_quote: Implementation has been delayed for activities under IPF sub-component I of the Program due to Ministry of Finance counter-funding delays, despite a later waiver by the Bank.

risk_title: Health facility infrastructure rehabilitation in conflict-affected areas stalled
risk_summary: Physical maintenance and rehabilitation of damaged health infrastructure in conflict-affected regions is significantly behind schedule, with only 49 of 129 facilities under renovation and 47 contracts requiring rebidding.
evidence_quote: Implementation has been delayed for activities under IPF sub-component I of the Program due to Ministry of Finance counter-funding delays, despite a later waiver by the Bank. However, according to MOH's report, renovation activities for 129 facilities across five regions are in progress, with 49 currently being renovated, 33 contracts awarded and 47 undergoing rebidding due to insufficient or unqualified bidders.

risk_title: Health service restoration in conflict-affected areas severely lagging
risk_summary: Health facilities in conflict-affected areas have not resumed services to pre-conflict status, with only 2 facilities achieving this milestone against a target of 262 by program closing.
evidence_quote: Implementation has been delayed for activities under IPF sub-component I of the Program due to Ministry of Finance counter-funding delays, despite a later waiver by the Bank.

risk_title: Postnatal care services declining in low-performing regions
risk_summary: Postnatal care service coverage in the four lowest-performing regions (Afar, Benishangul Gumuz, Gambella, Somali) has declined from baseline of 64% to current 52%, attributed to insufficient delivery waiting rooms, low community awareness, budget shortages, and high staff turnover in conflict-affected areas.
evidence_quote: The current achievement of 52% for 2024 not only misses the target but also represents a decline from the baseline of 64%. This was primarily attributed to the insufficient number of deliveries waiting rooms, low community awareness regarding service utilization, budget shortages, and high staff turnover, all of which require strengthening to improve service uptake in these low performing regions.
